title: श्री कुबेर कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री कुबेर कवचम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
गदापद्मनिधिश्रेष्ठं शङ्खपद्मनिधिप्रियम्।
कुबेरं पूजयेद्भक्त्या धनाध्यक्षं यशस्करम्॥

॥ कवचम् ॥
शिरो मे पातु यक्षेशो ललाटं धनदोऽवतु।
भ्रुवौ रक्षतु विश्वेशो नेत्रे रक्षतु शङ्खनिधिः॥ १॥

कर्णौ पातु निधीशश्च नासिकां पद्मनिधिपः।
मुखं पातु महायक्षो जिह्वां रक्षतु वित्तदः॥ २॥

कण्ठं रक्षतु राजेन्द्रो बाहू रक्षतु किन्नरेशः।
करौ पातु महालक्ष्मीपतिः हृदयमीश्वरः॥ ३॥

उदरं पातु लोकेशो नाभिं रक्षतु गुह्यकेशः।
कटिं पातु धनाध्यक्षः ऊरू पातु महाबलः॥ ४॥

जानुनी पातु भूतेशो जङ्घे रक्षतु कोशकृत्।
पादौ पातु नरेन्द्रेशः सर्वाङ्गं पातु वैश्रवणः॥ ५॥

प्राच्यां रक्षतु धनदो दक्षिणे यक्षकिन्नरः।
प्रतीच्यां रक्षतु कुबेरो उदीच्यां निधिनायकः॥ ६॥

ऊर्ध्वं रक्षतु सर्वेशो ह्यधस्तात्पातु शङ्करप्रियः।
सर्वतः सर्वकालं च कुबेरः सम्पदां पतिः॥ ७॥

॥ फलश्रुतिः ॥
कुबेरकवचं यो वै पठेद्भक्तिसमन्वितः।
अष्टैश्वर्ययुतो भूत्वा धनधान्यसमन्वितः॥

न दारिद्र्यं भवेत्तस्य कुबेरस्य प्रसादतः।
सर्वसम्पत्समृद्धिं च लभते नात्र संशयः॥

॥ इति श्री कुबेर कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Kubera Kavacham ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Gadapadmanidhishreshtham Shankhapadmanidhipriyam.
Kuberam Pujayedbhaktya Dhanadhyaksham Yashaskaram.

|| Kavacham ||
Shiro Me Patu Yakshesho Lalatam Dhanadovatu.
Bhruvau Rakshatu Vishvesho Netre Rakshatu Shankhanidhih. || 1 ||

Karnau Patu Nidhishashcha Nasikam Padmanidhipah.
Mukham Patu Mahayaksho Jihvam Rakshatu Vittadah. || 2 ||

Kantham Rakshatu Rajendro Bahu Rakshatu Kinnareshah.
Karau Patu Mahalakshmiipatih Hridayamishvarah. || 3 ||

Udaram Patu Lokesho Nabhim Rakshatu Guhyakeshah.
Katim Patu Dhanadhyakshah Uru Patu Mahabalah. || 4 ||

Januni Patu Bhutesho Janghe Rakshatu Koshakrit.
Padau Patu Narendreshah Sarvangam Patu Vaishravanah. || 5 ||

Prachyam Rakshatu Dhanado Dakshine Yakshakinnarah.
Pratichyam Rakshatu Kubero Udichyam Nidhinayakah. || 6 ||

Urdhvam Rakshatu Sarvesho Hyadhastatpatu Shankarapriyah.
Sarvatah Sarvakalam Cha Kuberah Sampadam Patih. || 7 ||

|| Phalashrutih ||
Kuberakavacham Yo Vai Pathed Bhaktisamanvitah.
Ashtaishvaryayuto Bhutva Dhanadhanyasamanvitah.

Na Daridryam Bhavettasya Kuberasya Prasadatah.
Sarvasampatsamriddhim Cha Labhate Natra Samshayah.

|| Iti Shri Kubera Kavacham Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning: कुबेर कवचम् में धनाध्यक्ष कुबेर द्वारा शरीर के प्रत्येक अंग और सभी दिशाओं की रक्षा का वर्णन है। शिर की रक्षा यक्षेश, ललाट की धनद, नेत्रों की शंखनिधि, मुख की महायक्ष, हृदय की ईश्वर, उदर की लोकेश और सर्वांग की वैश्रवण करते हैं। चारों दिशाओं में धनद, यक्षकिन्नर, कुबेर और निधिनायक रक्षा करते हैं। भक्तिपूर्वक पाठ करने वाला अष्टैश्वर्ययुक्त, धनधान्यसम्पन्न होता है और कभी दरिद्रता नहीं आती।
benefits: Traditionally invoked to establish divine armor of lord kubera, treasurer of gods
Invokes the blessing of ashta aishwarya - eight forms of divine wealth
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty permanently by kubera's grace
Invokes abundance of wealth, grains, and prosperity
Protects all body parts and directions through divine shield
description: Kubera Kavacham — श्री कुबेर कवचम् — is a divine armor prayer for protection offered to Lord Kubera. This revered text originates from Vishnu Dharmottara Purana. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to protect all body parts and directions through divine shield as well as remove poverty permanently by Kubera's grace. Additionally, it is known to grant abundance of wealth, grains, and prosperity. It is especially recommended on Friday (Shukravar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Dhanteras for maximum spiritual benefit. Comprising 7 verses, it can be completed in approximately 7 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Kubera Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Kubera Kavacham and who is Lord Kubera?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kubera Kavacham (श्री कुबेर कवचम्) is a sacred kavacha (armor) prayer dedicated to Lord Kubera — the Yaksharaja, treasurer of the gods, lord of the eight nidhis (great treasures), regent of the northern direction, and dhanadhyaksha (overseer of all wealth) in Hindu cosmology. The hymn is preserved in classical tradition (linked to Vishnu Dharmottara Purana corpus) and is structured in the classical kavacha format: a dhyana opening, anga-nyasa verses invoking different forms of Kubera to protect each part of the body, dik-rakshana verses for the eight directions, and a phalashruti promising ashta-aishwarya, removal of poverty, and abundance of wealth and grains for one who recites it with devotion. Kubera is the brother of Ravana, devotee of Shiva and friend of Lakshmi, and is invoked across all wealth pujas in Hindu households and businesses. (Sources: Vishnu Dharmottara Purana corpus; classical Kubera-puja tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the structure of the Kubera Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kubera Kavacham follows the classical kavacha (armor) structure used in Hindu protective stotras. (1) Dhyanam: a meditative opening describing Kubera as bearing the gada (mace), padma-nidhi and shankha-nidhi, dhanadhyaksha and yashaskara. (2) Anga-rakshana (verses 1-5): each part of the body — head, forehead, eyebrows, eyes, ears, nose, mouth, tongue, throat, arms, hands, heart, abdomen, navel, waist, thighs, knees, calves, feet, and the entire body — is protected by a different form of Kubera (Yakshesha, Dhanada, Vishvesha, Shankhanidhi, Padmanidhi, Mahayaksha, Vittada, Rajendra, Kinnaresha, Mahalakshmi-pati, Ishvara, Lokesha, Guhyakesha, Dhanadhyaksha, Mahabala, Bhutesha, Koshakrit, Narendresha, Vaishravana). (3) Dik-rakshana (verses 6-7): the eight directions and zenith-nadir are protected. (4) Phalashruti: promising ashta-aishwarya and removal of daridrya. (Sources: Kubera Kavacham complete; classical kavacha-paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the eight forms of wealth (ashta-aishwarya) granted by Kubera?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Kubera Kavacham promises ashtaishvarya-yutah — endowment with the eight kinds of aishwarya (divine prosperity). The classical eight aishwaryas in Hindu tradition include: (1) Dhana — gold, silver and currency wealth; (2) Dhanya — abundance of grains and food security; (3) Putra — virtuous offspring; (4) Bhumi — land and real estate; (5) Vahana — vehicles and transportation; (6) Vidya — knowledge and learning; (7) Yasha — fame, reputation and honour; (8) Bala — strength, health and influence. Kubera, as the lord of the nine nidhis (Padma, Mahapadma, Shankha, Makara, Kacchapa, Mukunda, Kunda, Nila, Kharva), bestows these aishwaryas on devotees who recite the kavacha with bhakti and combine it with ethical livelihood (dharmika-dhana). (Sources: Vishnu Dharmottara Purana; classical aishwarya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Kubera Kavacham be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Kubera Kavacham is most powerful when recited facing north (Kubera's direction) at Brahma muhurta or after the evening sandhya, after bath, before a Kubera or Lakshmi-Kubera yantra/picture, on a clean asana, with concentration on prosperity through dharmic means. It is especially auspicious on Fridays (Shukravar — sacred to Lakshmi-Kubera-Shukra), Thursdays (Guruvar — sacred to Brihaspati who blesses dhana-yoga), every Akshaya Tritiya (Vaishakha Shukla Tritiya — the supreme wealth day), Dhanteras (Karttika Krishna Trayodashi — the day Kubera is worshipped), Diwali Lakshmi Puja, Dhana Trayodashi, and on every Pushya Nakshatra day. Devotees often combine the kavacha with the Sri Suktam, Lakshmi Ashtakam and Kubera Ashtottara, recited 11, 21 or 108 times during financial difficulty or for business prosperity. (Sources: classical Kubera-puja paddhati; Lakshmi-Kubera yantra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Kubera Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Kubera Kavacham itself promises four powerful results: ashtaishvarya-yutah (endowment with the eight kinds of divine wealth); dhana-dhanya-samanvitah (abundance of money and grain); na daridryam bhavet tasya (poverty shall never touch the devotee); sarva-sampat-samriddhim (full prosperity in all forms). Traditional teachers add the following benefits: protection of the body and household from financial losses and theft through the kavacha shield; removal of obstacles in business, employment and inherited property disputes; clearance of long-standing debts (rina-mochana); awakening of the devotee's own dharmika-purushartha for earning wealth; favourable planetary support from Shukra (wealth) and Brihaspati (treasury); and the supreme phala of attaining the company of Lakshmi-Kubera in this life and Kubera-loka after death. (Sources: stotra phalashruti; classical Kubera tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Kubera Kavacham different from the Kubera Ashtottara Shatanamavali?
seoFaqs.answer: Both stotras invoke the same deity Kubera but differ in structure and purpose. Kubera Ashtottara Shatanamavali is a 108-name stotra listing Kubera's sacred names (Yaksharaja, Vaishravana, Dhanadhyaksha, Nidhipati, etc.) in the namavali format Om Kuberaya Namah, Om Vaishravanaya Namah, used principally for nama-japa and archana with flowers/akshata at each name. The Kubera Kavacham, by contrast, is a structured protective armor (kavacha) where each verse assigns a specific form of Kubera to guard a specific part of the body and direction, used principally for shielding, removal of poverty, and continuous protection of wealth. The two are complementary: Ashtottara is offered during shodashopachara puja, and the Kavacham is recited daily for ongoing protection and prosperity. (Sources: classical Kubera ritual paddhati.)
